# Implementation Plan: Phase 3B - Real-Time Feed Monitoring & Alerts

**Branch**: `003-real-time-monitoring` | **Date**: 2025-10-22 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)  
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/003-real-time-monitoring/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` workflow for Phase 3B implementation.

---

## Summary

Enable users to receive instant notifications when followed feeds publish new content or when trending topics emerge. Transform AIWebFeeds from a static catalog into a proactive content discovery platform with real-time monitoring, WebSocket-based push notifications, and intelligent alerting. Core features include background feed polling (15-60 min intervals), WebSocket real-time notifications (<60s latency), trending topic detection (z-score ≥2), and email digests (daily/weekly).

**Technical Approach**: APScheduler for background job scheduling, Socket.IO for WebSocket communication, SQLite for data persistence, self-hosted SMTP for email delivery, localStorage-based user identity (anonymous users). Feed polling uses httpx async client with 10 parallel workers. Trending detection compares against 7-day baseline with 3-day minimum for new topics.

---

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.13+ (backend, polling, WebSocket server), TypeScript 5.9+ (frontend WebSocket client, notification UI)  
**Primary Dependencies**:
- **Backend**: APScheduler (MIT, background jobs), Socket.IO (MIT, WebSocket), feedparser (BSD, feed parsing), httpx (async HTTP), tenacity (retry logic), Loguru (logging), tqdm (progress bars)
- **Frontend**: Socket.IO client (WebSocket), React 19+ (notification UI), Next.js 15+ (web framework)
- **Email**: Self-hosted SMTP (Postfix for production), SendGrid (dev/staging only)

**Storage**: SQLite (existing `data/aiwebfeeds.db`) with JSON1 extension (notification metadata), FTS5 (article full-text search), triggers (maintain health scores), WAL mode (concurrency)

**Testing**: pytest (unit/integration/E2E), pytest-cov (≥90% coverage), Hypothesis (property-based), mock WebSocket connections, time mocking for digest tests

**Target Platform**: Linux server (Ubuntu 22.04+), single-server deployment initially, horizontal scaling deferred until >1000 concurrent WebSocket connections

**Project Type**: Hybrid monorepo (Python backend + TypeScript web frontend)

**Performance Goals**:
- Feed polling: 10,000 feeds within 1 hour (30-minute average interval)
- WebSocket delivery: <60 seconds notification latency (95th percentile)
- Trending detection: Process within 5 minutes of article ingestion
- Email digest: <2 minutes generation time per user
- WebSocket connections: 1000 concurrent connections without degradation

**Constraints**:
- No user accounts (localStorage-based anonymous user_id, Phase 3A deferred)
- SendGrid free tier: 100 emails/day (self-hosted SMTP for production)
- Single-server deployment (no Redis pub/sub initially, add at >1000 WebSocket connections)
- Browser Push API deferred to Phase 4 (focus on in-app + email notifications)
- Simple HTML email templates (no MJML for MVP)

**Scale/Scope**:
- 10,000 feeds monitored (Phase 1/2 baseline)
- 100-1,000 users initially (anonymous, localStorage-based)
- 100k articles stored (no entry limit, SQLite scales well)
- 1M notifications (7-day retention, hard delete after)
- 10 parallel feed polling workers (httpx async)

---

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

### I. Documentation-First Development ✅ PASS
- **Status**: COMPLIANT
- **Evidence**: Feature documented in `spec.md` with comprehensive requirements. Documentation will be added to `apps/web/content/docs/features/notifications.mdx` during implementation.
- **Action**: Create `.mdx` documentation pages for:
  - `/features/real-time-notifications.mdx` (user-facing feature docs)
  - `/development/websocket-architecture.mdx` (technical implementation)
  - `/guides/configuring-digests.mdx` (user guide for email digests)

### II. Component Isolation & Modularity ✅ PASS
- **Status**: COMPLIANT
- **Evidence**: Phase 3B adds new functionality to Core Package (`packages/ai_web_feeds/`) for polling/notifications, extends CLI (`apps/cli/`) with notification commands, and updates Web (`apps/web/`) with WebSocket client and notification UI.
- **Dependencies**:
  - Core Package: Add `polling.py`, `notifications.py`, `trending.py`, extend `storage.py` models
  - CLI: Add `aiwebfeeds notify` commands (preferences, send-test, digest)
  - Web: Add WebSocket client, notification center UI, digest subscription UI
- **No circular dependencies**: Core → CLI (orchestration), Web → Core (data consumption)

### III. Type Safety & Data Integrity ✅ PASS
- **Status**: COMPLIANT
- **Evidence**: All new modules will use comprehensive type hints (Python) and strict TypeScript mode (frontend). SQLite schemas enforce data integrity with CHECK constraints and foreign keys.
- **Validation**:
  - Python: Type hints on all functions, Pydantic v2 for notification models
  - TypeScript: Strict mode, WebSocket message types defined
  - Database: CHECK constraints on status enums, foreign key relationships
  - mypy strict mode enforcement in CI

### IV. Test-First Development ✅ PASS
- **Status**: COMPLIANT
- **Evidence**: Test suite will mirror source structure with ≥90% coverage. Unit tests will mock external boundaries (HTTP, WebSocket, SMTP). Integration tests will exercise actual SQLite database.
- **Coverage Targets**:
  - `polling.py`: 95% (critical feed fetching logic)
  - `notifications.py`: 90% (WebSocket delivery, bundling)
  - `trending.py`: 92% (z-score computation, baseline handling)
  - `storage.py` extensions: 88% (database queries)
- **Test Types**: Unit (mocked boundaries), integration (real SQLite), E2E (simulated feed updates → notification delivery)

### V. Data Schema Compliance ✅ PASS
- **Status**: COMPLIANT
- **Evidence**: New SQLite tables defined with explicit schemas (see Database Architecture section). All data validated via SQLModel models before insertion.
- **Tables**: `feed_entries`, `feed_poll_jobs`, `notifications`, `trending_topics`, `notification_preferences`, `email_digests`
- **Validation**: SQLModel model validation on all inserts, CHECK constraints in schema, foreign key enforcement enabled

### VI. Modern Stack Commitment ✅ PASS
- **Status**: COMPLIANT
- **Evidence**: All dependencies use latest stable versions: Python 3.13+, APScheduler 3.10+, Socket.IO 5.x, httpx 0.27+, Next.js 15+, React 19+.
- **Security**: Dependencies pinned in `uv.lock` and `pnpm-lock.yaml`, automated security scans in CI
- **Updates**: Dependency update plan: quarterly review, security patches within 30 days

### VII. Code Quality & Conventions ✅ PASS
- **Status**: COMPLIANT
- **Evidence**: All code will pass Ruff (Python) and ESLint 9 (TypeScript) checks. Conventional commits enforced. Absolute imports only.
- **Linting**: Pre-commit hooks for Ruff, ESLint, mypy
- **Commits**: Format `feat(notifications): add WebSocket delivery`, `fix(polling): handle feed redirect 301/302`
- **Imports**: Absolute imports from `ai_web_feeds` package root
- **Docstrings**: Google-style docstrings for all public functions

### Summary: ✅ ALL GATES PASS
- No constitutional violations detected
- All principles satisfied
- Complexity justified by Phase 3B scope (real-time features inherently complex)
- No exceptions required

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
# Hybrid monorepo structure (Python + TypeScript)

packages/ai_web_feeds/src/ai_web_feeds/
├── __init__.py
├── config.py                 # Existing (add notification settings)
├── models.py                 # Existing (add notification models)

├── email_digest.py           # NEW: Email digest generation

├── __init__.py

├── poll.py                   # NEW: Manual feed polling commands
└── digest.py                 # NEW: Digest testing commands

apps/web/
├── app/
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── notifications/
│   │   │   ├── route.ts      # NEW: Fetch user notifications (REST)
│   │   │   └── preferences/
│   │   │       └── route.ts  # NEW: Update notification preferences
│   │   ├── poll/
│   │   │   └── [id]/
│   │   │       └── route.ts  # NEW: Manual feed poll trigger (admin)
│   │   └── websocket/
│   │       └── route.ts      # NEW: WebSocket connection endpoint
│   ├── notifications/
│   │   ├── page.tsx          # NEW: Notification center page
│   │   └── preferences/
│   │       └── page.tsx      # NEW: Notification preferences UI
│   └── digests/
│       └── page.tsx          # NEW: Digest subscription page
├── components/
│   ├── NotificationBell.tsx  # NEW: Header notification icon
│   ├── NotificationCard.tsx  # NEW: Individual notification display
│   ├── NotificationCenter.tsx # NEW: Notification list panel
│   └── DigestForm.tsx        # NEW: Digest subscription form
└── lib/
    ├── websocket.ts          # NEW: WebSocket client wrapper
    └── notifications.ts      # NEW: Notification state management

tests/
├── packages/
│   └── ai_web_feeds/
│       ├── test_polling.py           # NEW: Feed polling tests
│       ├── test_notifications.py     # NEW: WebSocket delivery tests
│       ├── test_trending.py          # NEW: Trending detection tests
│       ├── test_email_digest.py      # NEW: Digest generation tests
│       └── integration/
│           ├── test_poll_to_notify.py # NEW: E2E poll → notify flow
│           └── test_trending_alerts.py # NEW: Trending detection integration
└── cli/
└── unit/
        ├── test_notify_commands.py   # NEW: CLI notification tests
        ├── test_poll_commands.py     # NEW: CLI polling tests
        └── test_digest_commands.py   # NEW: CLI digest tests

# Background services (deployment)
services/
├── polling_scheduler.py      # NEW: APScheduler service (systemd)
├── websocket_server.py       # NEW: Socket.IO server (systemd)
└── digest_scheduler.py       # NEW: Daily/weekly digest sender (systemd)
```

**Structure Decision**: Hybrid monorepo structure maintained. Core Package handles all business logic (polling, notifications, trending). CLI orchestrates with user-facing commands. Web provides real-time UI via WebSocket and notification center. Background services deployed as systemd units on production server.

---

## Complexity Tracking

**No violations requiring justification.** All complexity is inherent to real-time notification features and aligns with Phase 3B scope.

**Complexity Justifications**:

| Component | Complexity | Justification |
|-----------|------------|---------------|
| APScheduler | Background job queue | Required for scheduled feed polling without cron complexity. In-process scheduler avoids Redis dependency. |
| Socket.IO | WebSocket server | Proven mature library with auto-fallback (polling, SSE). Reduces implementation complexity vs native WebSocket + manual fallback. |
| Trending Detection | Statistical z-score computation | Standard algorithm for spike detection. Simpler alternatives (fixed threshold) have unacceptable false positive rates. |
| Email Digest | HTML template generation | Necessary for user-friendly digest emails. Plain text insufficient for article lists with links. |
| localStorage User ID | Anonymous user identification | Avoids Phase 3A (User Accounts) dependency. Simple UUID generation, server-side storage for reliability. |

**Alternatives Considered**:

| Alternative | Rejected Because |
|-------------|-----------------|
| **Celery + Redis** (vs APScheduler) | Redis adds infrastructure complexity. APScheduler sufficient for single-server deployment at target scale (10k feeds). |
| **Native WebSocket** (vs Socket.IO) | Requires manual fallback logic (SSE, long polling). Socket.IO provides auto-fallback and room-based broadcasting. |
| **PostgreSQL** (vs SQLite) | SQLite handles target scale (10k feeds, 1M notifications). PostgreSQL overkill for Phase 3B. Defer until multi-server deployment. |
| **Browser Push API** (vs defer to Phase 4) | Requires service worker complexity. In-app notifications + email digests provide 80% of value with 20% complexity. |
| **MJML Email Templates** (vs simple HTML) | MJML adds build complexity. Simple HTML table sufficient for MVP, accessible across email clients. |

---

## Phase 0: Research (Completed)

**Status**: ✅ COMPLETE - See [clarifications.md](./clarifications.md) for detailed decisions.

**Key Decisions**:
1. **User Identity**: localStorage UUID (anonymous users), migrate to Phase 3A later
2. **Background Jobs**: APScheduler (no Redis for MVP)
3. **WebSocket**: Socket.IO (mature, auto-fallback)
4. **Email Delivery**: Self-hosted SMTP (production) + SendGrid (dev/staging)
5. **Browser Push**: Deferred to Phase 4
6. **Trending Baseline**: 3-day minimum for new topics
7. **Feed Entries**: Store all articles (no limit, SQLite scales)
8. **Notification Retention**: Hard delete after 7 days

**Output**: [research.md](./research.md) - Will be generated in next step
